contextBanner: [TBG | Stale PR Recovery Progress | harness/cleanup | branch: feat/stale-pr-progress-dashboard]
purpose: Convert the stale PR recovery manifest into deterministic instructions and maintain an evidence-backed per-PR progress ledger with a generated Markdown completion dashboard, without executing a cherry-pick, closing a PR, deleting a branch, or claiming runtime proof.
allowedScope: read the committed stale PR recovery plan
select a wave or source PR
read and validate the canonical per-PR progress ledger
update one explicitly selected progress entry through the set action
require disposition and evidence before a terminal completion status can be recorded
calculate aggregate complete, in-progress, blocked, and not-started counts
write the tracked Markdown dashboard and ignored progress result artifacts
write ignored result, report, event, progress, and handoff artifacts
block execution when local floor proof is absent
name exact validators and disposition gates
forbiddenScope: close or retarget a pull request
mark an open replacement pull request as terminal completion
infer completion from a PR state without a recorded disposition and evidence
claim build, behavior, or runtime proof

rules: Every sentence names the acting subject.
Every sentence contains one explicit action verb.
Every conditional sentence states the condition before the terminal decision.
Every command sentence names the evidence that the command must produce.
Every blocked state names one exact next command or one exact owner decision.
The English report and JSON result describe the same instruction objects.
The progress Markdown and progress JSON describe the same per-PR states and aggregate counts.
Raw JSON is never the primary human report.

terminalRequirements: a next action or explicit no-further-work statement
progressGate.completeRule: Every planned source PR has one terminal completion status.

requires: the progress ledger contains exactly the same source PRs as the plan
every instruction is a complete sentence
the JSON and English instruction artifacts contain the same instruction count
the progress JSON and Markdown contain matching aggregate state
terminal dispositions fail closed without evidence
an open replacement PR remains non-terminal
all planned entries must be terminal before the dashboard reports COMPLETE
no generated command uses a destructive forbidden operation
a non-floor wave blocks without local floor proof
a verified-floor fixture produces a bounded ready decision
